title: Loss of Lance of Longinus Alleviates Liliths Lack of Legs
display_title: Loss of Lance of Longinus Alleviates Liliths Lack of Legs
wikitext:
[]: Loss of Lance of Longinus Alleviates Lilith's Lack of Legs             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jun 30, 2010 3:49 am We know from the Laserdisc liner notes that Unit 01 and Lilith were still connected when Yui's contact experiment with Unit 01 took place. More specifically, Unit 01 was being grown from Lilith's legs. When Yui vanished into Unit 01, the salvage operation failed and the result was a bunch of Reis. Most were soulless, but one somehow ended up with Lilith's soul. Lilith, once no longer attached to Unit 01, did not regenerate her old lower section. Instead, she grew a bunch of Rei legs. And butts. And maybe genitalia, but the artists weren't allowed to draw those. Anyway, this leads me to think that, due to the absence of Lilith's intrinsic soul and the contact with Yui's soul, Lilith's identity became "jumbled" with that of Yui. Lilith's body perceived itself as Rei's body, thus when it came to regrowing what it had lost, it grew in the form of Rei. Lilith remained like this for a while until Adam's Spear of Longinus was inserted into her in episode 15. Then she remained as she was except with a big fork sticking into her chest. Eventually, in episode 22, the Spear of Longinus was removed , and suddenly Lilith regenerated her old legs. So this means that Adam's Spear of Longinus, which we know from the CI is a living organism and has a "will", somehow restored Lilith's body's identity as Lilith's body upon contact. However, because impaling a SoL with the SoL results in the SoL going into suspended animation, Lilith did not regenerate her "true" self until the Spear was removed. Or something. Right?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jun 30, 2010 4:04 am But then why would she keep the extra legs, even after regenerating her own? I don't know... Isn't that too roundabout, even for Anno? What I always figured was that the reason Lilith regenerated her legs right then is because it reflected her body reaching its "final stage" the CI talks about -- an event which happens only then because Rei had been instructed to insert the Spear at just the proper time, just before Lilith would have gained the ability to regenerate like that. Of course, the DC version has the regeneration happen within seconds of the Spear's removal, which makes that seem less likely, I know. The timing is awfully convenient, true, but I think no less convenient than other things like Touji and Kensuke not getting squished during the Shamshel fight. Showing the regeneration happening right after the Spear's removal makes it clear that the removal of the Spear is exactly what allows it to happen, even if logically the odds are pretty slim that the regeneration should happen mere seconds later as opposed to hours or days. By the way, are we sure it's not possible that Lilith regrew her legs after detaching from Unit 01, and that her legs weren't removed again for some other reason after she was Speared? ... Actually, my brain just spontaneously created an even more likely idea: Perhaps Lilith didn't regenerate her legs earlier because she didn't have the resources (material, energy, etc) to do so. Perhaps Lilith was not hooked up to those weird tubes in her head and fed with what would allow the regeneration until after she was Speared, the reason being that they didn't want her to be 'whole' and Impact-ready until they wanted her to be. By the time she was un-Speared, she had accumulated more than enough necessary resources to perform the regeneration. ...Also, where can I see a copy of these liner notes?

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Wed Jun 30, 2010 4:14 am If I remember correctly, Rei paused slightly as she removed the Lance from Lillith. Since Rei II knew that she is connected to Lillith, is it possible that the reason why the legs suddenly 'regrew' (as opposed to staying as they were) a result of Rei being present? Is it possible that with the lance removed, Rei willed for Lillith's legs to regenerate? Just a thought.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jun 30, 2010 4:24 am EvangelionFan wrote: If I remember correctly, Rei paused slightly as she removed the Lance from Lillith. Since Rei II knew that she is connected to Lillith, is it possible that the reason why the legs suddenly 'regrew' (as opposed to staying as they were) a result of Rei being present? Is it possible that with the lance removed, Rei willed for Lillith's legs to regenerate? Just a thought.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jun 30, 2010 6:31 am NAveryW wrote: Right?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jun 30, 2010 6:47 am NemZ wrote: Sounds good to me. Side note: We never see Lilith with normal legs in the TV version. The entire regeneration thing is a retcon for EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jun 30, 2010 1:46 pm Monk Ed wrote: But then why would she keep the extra legs, even after regenerating her own?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jun 30, 2010 3:35 pm Naw-kun : Good theory and good thread. A+ for the day.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jun 30, 2010 4:22 pm There's a separate thread regarding the canonicity of the director's cuts here . About Lilith's ostensible lack of leg regeneration until the removal of the Spear of Longinus, the main point of my topic post was that I realized that she did regenerate legs. They just weren't her own; they were Rei's. Look closely at Lilith on the cross. Lilith's body ends where the Rei bodies begin. She wasn't prevented from regenerating her lost legs. Her body was somehow "confused" into thinking that it should regrow her lost body parts in the form of Rei. The Spear of Longinus (or Unit 00's contact with the Spear of Longinus) "restored" Lilith's identity, so when the Spear was removed, Lilith regrew her legs in her "true" form. Regardless of whether or not they regenerated at that specific moment, they were there by the time End of Evangelion rolled around.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Jun 30, 2010 10:01 pm The theory sounds pretty solid to me. Also, this thread title is truly a titanic treatment of alliterative titling!

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 01, 2010 1:42 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Also, this thread title is truly a titanic treatment of alliterative titling!

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jul 01, 2010 2:17 am Quick, edit the OP before anyone notices!

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jul 01, 2010 2:23 am How about "obviates" instead of "overwrites"?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Jul 01, 2010 8:42 pm NAveryW wrote: I should have called it "Loss of Lance of Longinus Overwrites Lilith's Ostensible Lack of Legs" because then its acronym would be LOLOLOLOLOL.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Thu Jul 01, 2010 9:40 pm That...that theory's actually quite brilliant. I'm impressed. You know, this also sheds light on why they thought Rei could pilot Eva-01....

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Tue Jul 13, 2010 1:20 am There is obviously significance in the fact they included the shot of Lilith's legs growing back in 22' and it definitely helps explain why Lilith morphed into a Giant Naked Rei rather than something else [Since Rei stated in 25 she was afraid that acknowledging her identity as Lilith would cause herself as Rei to cease to be, and we know quite well in EoE this isn't the case.] And Ritsuko's statement of 'Rei was the only one born with a soul' in 23. Great theory!
